questions.question: What is the core difference between `v-if` and `v-show`?
options: `v-if` works only on native elements; `v-show` works on components too
`v-if` adds/removes elements from the DOM; `v-show` toggles CSS `display: none`
`v-if` is evaluated once at mount; `v-show` is re-evaluated on every render
`v-if` supports multiple conditions; `v-show` only supports a boolean

questions.question: In Vue 3, what is the precedence when `v-if` and `v-for` are on the same element?
options: `v-for` has higher priority and runs before `v-if`
`v-if` has higher priority and runs before `v-for`, so it cannot access the loop variable
They have equal priority and Vue processes them left to right
Vue 3 throws a compile error when both directives are on the same element

questions.question: When is `v-show` preferable to `v-if`?
options: When the element should only render once on initial mount
When the element must not exist in the DOM for security reasons
When the user toggles the element frequently, since toggling `display` is cheaper than DOM removal/recreation
When the element contains a `<template>` wrapper with multiple children
